message:
fix(input): handle Tab/Enter/LF as proper key codes instead of Ctrl+letter

The input parser was treating special control characters as Ctrl+letter:
- Tab (0x09) was Ctrl+I
- LF (0x0A) was Ctrl+J
- CR (0x0D) was Ctrl+M

This is technically correct at the ASCII level, but not intuitive for
users who expect Tab to be Tab. Now these return their proper key codes:
- 0x09 -> KeyCode::Tab
- 0x0A -> KeyCode::Enter
- 0x0D -> KeyCode::Enter
- 0x00 -> KeyCode::Null with CTRL modifier

Updated tests to reflect this improved behavior.

Also closed 7 EPICs with completed child tasks:
- bd-affo: Color System Correctness
- bd-2qib: Input/Terminal System Robustness
- bd-3nbd: Text/Editor System Fixes
- bd-l4ig: Cell/Grapheme System Performance
- bd-3bxo: Buffer/Rendering System Improvements
- bd-tv2h: Unit Test Coverage Infrastructure
- bd-3k9i: demo_showcase Unit Test Expansion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
